activities_projects.title: Consecutive Number Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Explore patterns in sums of consecutive numbers
steps: Write five sets of four consecutive numbers
Insert '+' and '-' signs between them in different ways
Calculate the results and observe patterns
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculations and pattern explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Parity Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Understand even and odd number patterns
steps: Write ten arithmetic expressions using even and odd numbers
Calculate the results
Identify whether the results are even or odd

activities_projects.title: Always, Sometimes, Never Activity
steps: Write five mathematical statements about factors and multiples
Test them with different numbers
Classify each statement as always true, sometimes true or never true

activities_projects.title: Divisibility Rule Investigation
steps: Write ten numbers divisible by 9
Add their digits
Observe the relationship between the sum of digits and divisibility

activities_projects.title: Algebraic Expression Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify expressions that always produce even numbers
steps: Write algebraic expressions like 2n, 4m + 2q and 3x + 2
Substitute different integer values
Identify which expressions always produce even numbers

activities_projects.title: Multiple of Four Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Investigate patterns in multiples of four
steps: Choose pairs of even numbers
Add them together
Check when the sum becomes a multiple of four

activities_projects.title: Remainder Pattern Exploration
steps: Write numbers that leave remainder 3 when divided by 5
Identify the pattern
Express the pattern using algebraic form

activities_projects.title: Expression Parity Project
steps: Write expressions like a+b, a-b and a+b+c
Substitute even and odd numbers
Analyse the parity of results

activities_projects.title: Divisibility Logic Puzzle
steps: Create five puzzles involving divisibility rules
Exchange puzzles with classmates
Solve and explain the solutions

activities_projects.title: Number Pattern Research
steps: Choose a number pattern involving addition or subtraction
Test the pattern with multiple numbers
Explain the pattern using algebraic reasoning
